behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary is the scoring rule logic itself: config lookup by merchant category plus threshold-to-score calculation. No real I/O or cross-module wiring is required to expose an incorrect score computation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring function/object with an in-memory config (category threshold map) and a transaction just above the category threshold; assert the exact numeric score contribution and final score. Include a boundary case at exactly threshold vs above threshold to catch off-by-one comparator bugs.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Correctness here is model judgment quality (pattern recognition without explicit threshold triggers), not just deterministic wiring. A mocked/stubbed model would hide true misclassification behavior of the real model.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on a curated fraud-pattern fixture set where each transaction stays under individual scoring thresholds but the sequence/context indicates fraud (geo spread, rapid cadence, device signals). Assert fraud probability exceeds alert threshold (or classification is fraud) for known positive cases, with acceptance criteria over repeated runs to handle model variance.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: The real failure commonly appears at module boundaries: transaction retrieval plus time-window filtering (timestamp precision, inclusive/exclusive bounds, ordering). Pure unit tests of counting logic can miss repository/query and boundary-time interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Using a local test DB (or filesystem-backed event store), seed per-user transactions around window edges (inside, exactly on boundary, just outside). Execute the scoring service velocity check through its normal repository/query path and assert returned count and resulting score component.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: This behavior validates orchestration across two model outputs and hold-decision logic. The failure mode is incorrect wiring/composition (AND vs OR), which is a multi-module interaction boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real scoring + risk decision modules (with deterministic test doubles only for model outputs if needed) in the transaction evaluation service. Feed a case where scoring says not flagged and risk says flagged; assert hold-for-review is true. Add converse and both-false controls to verify full OR truth table at service boundary.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: This is an output contract assembled from multiple components (scoring result + risk result + reviewer payload). Real failures are field loss/mis-mapping/type mismatch at composition boundaries, not single-function arithmetic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the evaluation pipeline through the response-assembly layer with both model outputs present. Assert the emitted review payload contains numeric `risk_score`, `fraud_probability`, and model-specific flag reasons mapped to the correct fields/types. Validate contract against schema to catch serialization/mapping regressions.
